batches.name: infrastructure
batches.description: Parser struct, advance/peek/match/expect/pos helpers, lexer, AST node definitions

batches.name: names
batches.description: Identifiers, "quoted" identifiers, qualified names schema.object@dblink, bind variables :param

batches.name: types
batches.description: NUMBER, VARCHAR2, CLOB, BLOB, DATE, TIMESTAMP, INTERVAL, user-defined object types, %TYPE, %ROWTYPE

batches.name: expressions
batches.description: Operators, CASE, DECODE, BETWEEN, IN, LIKE/LIKEC, IS, NVL, NVL2, COALESCE, NULLIF, CAST, MULTISET

batches.name: select
batches.description: SELECT with CONNECT BY, START WITH, PIVOT/UNPIVOT, FLASHBACK, SAMPLE, FOR UPDATE, FETCH FIRST

batches.name: insert
batches.description: INSERT, INSERT ALL/FIRST, multi-table insert, RETURNING

batches.name: update_delete
batches.description: UPDATE, DELETE, RETURNING INTO

batches.name: merge
batches.description: MERGE INTO

batches.name: create_table
batches.description: CREATE TABLE, partitioning, storage, LOB storage, virtual columns, invisible columns

batches.name: alter_table
batches.description: ALTER TABLE, ADD/MODIFY/DROP/RENAME COLUMN, constraints, partitions

batches.name: create_index
batches.description: CREATE INDEX, BITMAP, FUNCTION-BASED, LOCAL/GLOBAL, REVERSE

batches.name: create_view
batches.description: CREATE VIEW, MATERIALIZED VIEW, FORCE/NO FORCE

batches.name: sequence_synonym
batches.description: CREATE SEQUENCE, CREATE SYNONYM, CREATE DATABASE LINK

batches.name: create_proc
batches.description: CREATE PROCEDURE/FUNCTION, packages: CREATE PACKAGE / PACKAGE BODY

batches.name: create_trigger
batches.description: CREATE TRIGGER: DML, DDL, system, compound, INSTEAD OF

batches.name: create_type
batches.description: CREATE TYPE: object types, nested tables, varrays

batches.name: drop
batches.description: DROP TABLE/INDEX/VIEW/SEQUENCE/SYNONYM/PACKAGE/PROCEDURE/FUNCTION/TRIGGER/TYPE, PURGE

batches.name: alter_misc
batches.description: ALTER INDEX, ALTER VIEW, ALTER SEQUENCE, ALTER SESSION, ALTER SYSTEM

batches.name: grant
batches.description: GRANT, REVOKE, CREATE/ALTER/DROP USER/ROLE

batches.name: transaction
batches.description: COMMIT, ROLLBACK, SAVEPOINT, SET TRANSACTION

batches.name: comment
batches.description: COMMENT ON, hints parsing

batches.name: plsql_block
batches.description: DECLARE, BEGIN/END, IF/ELSIF/ELSE, LOOP/WHILE/FOR, EXCEPTION, RETURN, GOTO

batches.name: utility
batches.description: ANALYZE, TRUNCATE, EXPLAIN PLAN, FLASHBACK TABLE, PURGE

batches.name: stmt_dispatch
batches.description: Wire up all statements in the parseStmt dispatcher

batches.name: select_pivot_unpivot
batches.description: Fix batch 4 gap: PIVOT and UNPIVOT clauses are listed in batch 4 rules but not implemented. PIVOT rotates rows into columns; UNPIVOT does the reverse. These are Oracle-specific SELECT clauses parsed after FROM.

batches.name: select_model
batches.description: Fix batch 4 gap: MODEL clause is listed in batch 4 rules but not implemented. The MODEL clause enables spreadsheet-like calculations in SQL with PARTITION BY, DIMENSION BY, MEASURES, and RULES.

batches.name: select_sample_flashback
batches.description: Fix batch 4 gap: SAMPLE clause and FLASHBACK query (AS OF SCN/TIMESTAMP, VERSIONS BETWEEN) are listed in batch 4 rules but not implemented. SAMPLE returns a random sample of rows. FLASHBACK queries retrieve historical data.

batches.name: analytic_functions
batches.description: Analytic/window functions: OVER clause with PARTITION BY, ORDER BY, and windowing (ROWS/RANGE BETWEEN). Also KEEP (DENSE_RANK FIRST/LAST) and WITHIN GROUP for aggregate functions like LISTAGG.

batches.name: grouping_extensions
batches.description: GROUP BY extensions: GROUPING SETS, CUBE, ROLLUP. These are Oracle extensions for multi-dimensional aggregation within the GROUP BY clause.

batches.name: lateral_xmltable_jsontable
batches.description: LATERAL inline views, XMLTABLE, and JSON_TABLE as table sources in FROM clause. LATERAL allows correlated subqueries in FROM. XMLTABLE/JSON_TABLE convert XML/JSON data to relational rows.

batches.name: oracle_join_syntax
batches.description: Oracle legacy (+) outer join syntax in WHERE clause conditions. The (+) operator after a column reference indicates the side that may have nulls.

batches.name: cursor_expressions
batches.description: Cursor expressions: CURSOR(subquery) in SELECT list. Also MULTISET operations (MULTISET UNION, MULTISET INTERSECT, MULTISET EXCEPT) and TREAT expression for type casting in object hierarchies.

batches.name: xml_expressions
batches.description: XML expression functions: XMLELEMENT, XMLFOREST, XMLAGG, XMLROOT, XMLPARSE, XMLSERIALIZE. These generate or transform XML data within SQL expressions.

batches.name: json_expressions
batches.description: JSON expression functions: JSON_OBJECT, JSON_ARRAY, JSON_QUERY, JSON_VALUE, JSON_EXISTS, JSON_MERGEPATCH, IS JSON condition. Oracle 23c JSON features.

batches.name: lock_table
batches.description: LOCK TABLE statement: LOCK TABLE table IN {ROW SHARE|ROW EXCLUSIVE|SHARE|SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE|EXCLUSIVE} MODE [NOWAIT|WAIT n].

batches.name: call_stmt
batches.description: CALL statement for invoking stored procedures/functions: CALL [schema.]routine_name([args]) [INTO :bind_variable].

batches.name: rename_stmt
batches.description: RENAME statement: RENAME old_name TO new_name. Top-level DDL statement for renaming tables, views, sequences, and synonyms.

batches.name: set_role_set_constraint
batches.description: SET ROLE and SET CONSTRAINT(S) session control statements. SET ROLE enables/disables roles. SET CONSTRAINT(S) changes deferrable constraint behavior.

batches.name: audit_noaudit
batches.description: AUDIT and NOAUDIT statements for unified and traditional auditing. AUDIT tracks SQL operations; NOAUDIT disables auditing.

batches.name: associate_statistics
batches.description: ASSOCIATE STATISTICS and DISASSOCIATE STATISTICS statements for binding statistics types to database objects.

batches.name: create_user_role_profile
batches.description: Fix batch 18 gap: CREATE/ALTER/DROP USER, ROLE, PROFILE are listed in batch 18 rules but not implemented (only GRANT/REVOKE are). Full CREATE USER with IDENTIFIED BY, DEFAULT TABLESPACE, etc. CREATE ROLE, ALTER ROLE, DROP ROLE. CREATE/ALTER/DROP PROFILE with resource limits.

batches.name: create_tablespace_directory
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP TABLESPACE, CREATE/DROP DIRECTORY, CREATE/DROP CONTEXT. Administrative DDL for storage and namespace management.

batches.name: create_cluster_dimension
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP CLUSTER (table clustering), CREATE/ALTER/DROP DIMENSION (OLAP dimension objects), CREATE/DROP FLASHBACK ARCHIVE.

batches.name: create_java_library
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP JAVA (Java source/class/resource), CREATE/ALTER/DROP LIBRARY (external C library). Less common DDL for external code integration.

batches.name: create_mview_log
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW LOG. MV logs track changes to base tables enabling fast refresh of materialized views.

batches.name: alter_plsql_objects
batches.description: ALTER PROCEDURE, ALTER FUNCTION, ALTER TRIGGER, ALTER TYPE, ALTER PACKAGE, ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW, ALTER DATABASE LINK, ALTER SYNONYM. Currently ALTER INDEX/VIEW/SEQUENCE use a generic skip-to-semicolon stub; these need proper object-specific parsing.

batches.name: drop_extended
batches.description: Fix batch 16 gap: extend DROP to handle DROP USER, DROP ROLE, DROP PROFILE, DROP TABLESPACE, DROP DIRECTORY, DROP CONTEXT, DROP CLUSTER, DROP DIMENSION, DROP FLASHBACK ARCHIVE, DROP JAVA, DROP LIBRARY, DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW LOG, DROP DATABASE.

batches.name: plsql_forall_bulk
batches.description: PL/SQL FORALL statement for bulk DML operations, and BULK COLLECT INTO in SELECT INTO. Also EXIT [WHEN] and CONTINUE [WHEN] loop control statements which are missing from batch 21.

batches.name: plsql_pipe_pragma
batches.description: PL/SQL PIPE ROW statement for pipelined table functions, and PRAGMA directives (AUTONOMOUS_TRANSACTION, EXCEPTION_INIT, RESTRICT_REFERENCES, SERIALLY_REUSABLE, UDF, INLINE). Also PL/SQL CASE statement (distinct from CASE expression).

batches.name: plsql_collections_records
batches.description: PL/SQL collection type declarations in DECLARE: TYPE name IS TABLE OF, TYPE name IS VARRAY, TYPE name IS RECORD. Also associative array (INDEX BY) and REF CURSOR declarations.

batches.name: plsql_procedure_call
batches.description: Fix batch 21 gap: PL/SQL procedure call statements currently return nil in parsePLSQLAssignOrCall. Need a PLSQLCall AST node for standalone procedure invocations, and support for named parameter notation (param => value).

batches.name: create_table_partitioning
batches.description: Fix batch 8 gap: partitioning (PARTITION BY RANGE/LIST/HASH), storage clause, LOB storage are listed in batch 8 rules but implemented only as stubs. Full RANGE/LIST/HASH/COMPOSITE partitioning syntax with VALUES LESS THAN, VALUES, subpartitions.

batches.name: alter_index_view_sequence_full
batches.description: Fix batch 17 gap: ALTER INDEX, ALTER VIEW, ALTER SEQUENCE currently use parseAlterGeneric which skips to semicolon and returns a placeholder AlterSessionStmt. Need proper AST nodes and parsing for ALTER INDEX REBUILD/RENAME/MONITORING, ALTER VIEW COMPILE, ALTER SEQUENCE options.

batches.name: create_schema
batches.description: CREATE SCHEMA statement which allows creating multiple tables, views, and grants in a single transaction.

batches.name: flashback_database
batches.description: FLASHBACK DATABASE statement: FLASHBACK DATABASE TO {SCN|TIMESTAMP|RESTORE POINT}. Distinct from FLASHBACK TABLE (batch 22).

batches.name: exists_subquery_fix
batches.description: Fix batch 3 gap: EXISTS expression currently skips parenthesized content with a depth counter instead of properly parsing the subquery. Need to parse the inner SELECT statement and store it in ExistsExpr.Subquery. Also fix parseParenExpr to detect and delegate subquery expressions to parseSelectStmt.

batches.name: compound_trigger
batches.description: Fix batch 14 gap: compound triggers (BEFORE STATEMENT, BEFORE EACH ROW, AFTER EACH ROW, AFTER STATEMENT sections) and DDL/database event triggers (ON DATABASE/SCHEMA) are listed in batch 14 description but not implemented.

batches.name: stmt_dispatch_extended
batches.description: Wire up new statement types in parseStmt dispatcher: LOCK TABLE, CALL, RENAME, SET ROLE, SET CONSTRAINTS, AUDIT, NOAUDIT.
